sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"3ce6a755-de3c-428a-bf94-dce5e099ced6","component":"Text","data":{"content":"The obvious answer supported, easy to parse, and used across almost every modern ecosystem. So JSON became the storage strategy.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"c6590554-bf78-471f-846d-4600d268b3cb","component":"Text","data":{"content":"But then another problem appeared: searching content efficiently.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"bd94b60d-4c43-4530-b64a-25d84faaf96d","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Iterating through separate files wouldn’t work well. Each line is its own JSON object, which means the system can stream data line-by-line, parse it quickly, apply filters, and paginate results efficiently.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"01454a4e-3119-4765-a294-a374291ddca1","component":"Text","data":{"content":"It’s incredibly simple and extremely fast.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"ac0eb5ef-cdc9-41f0-8be4-5c883e939bbe","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"line","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"e8b0c476-9fc1-4c1e-90a5-db493b20c287","component":"Text","data":{"content":"The Page System","tag":"h4","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"8e168d5d-6ede-478f-92a1-1175e3cc769d","component":"Text","data":{"content":"At a previous company, we used JSON to define page structure.  It was a pattern I really liked because it made the UI extremely flexible. Components could be composed easily, and data could be passed around cleanly using providers and hooks.  So I decided to use the same idea in CodeFolio.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"609494bc-6098-44ae-bded-d28c37f88705","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Pages are built from JSON-defined component trees. The format is clean, self-documenting, and easy to migrate if someone ever wants to move their data elsewhere.  Your content is always portable.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"1e323f51-c38a-4908-8737-3b7165d21a23","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"line","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"493c6094-8a02-4018-ab07-ab9ceefdf730","component":"Text","data":{"content":"The Canvas","tag":"h4","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"7ed1dd8a-a4da-46c1-be33-170a0120d367","component":"Text","data":{"content":"To make this work, I built a Canvas renderer.  The canvas recursively renders a component tree from JSON using a component registry.  Once that existed, something interesting happened.  It meant I could also build a drag and drop visual editor on top of it.  And because components are registered centrally, it also opens the door to:","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"6842de02-e89b-4d6e-969a-8793d3a28419","component":"List","data":{"title":"","variant":"unordered","divided":false,"className":""},"children":[{"id":"540e8e93-8b11-42d7-b505-75b9a5df8be3","component":"ListItem","data":{"className":""},"children":[{"id":"17ac4885-3a9e-4f1e-92a4-df8d89c0517d","component":"Text","data":{"content":" - I separated first-party and third-party code so updates would be easy to manage.  Then I thought:","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"0226113a-e48e-44e5-8e1c-9cfc9e906a0e","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Why not let users update directly from the dashboard?","tag":"i","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"14px","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"b24e0830-6283-4a6f-ad70-66326c78df48","component":"Text","data":{"content":"The admin server runs on Express, and serves directories exactly the way a static site would. That means the transition from editing locally to deploying a static site is almost frictionless.  Under the hood, the admin panel follows a Controller / Service / Entity structure, with decorators and a pipeline event bus that allows plugins or domain logic to modify entities when needed.  It ended up being far more powerful than I originally planned.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"289dab6e-5ab3-40be-a6ec-abe2594e9a9e","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"line","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"6a95cf22-2b62-4f7a-8994-61b2150dfa1f","component":"Text","data":{"content":"The First Deployment","tag":"h4","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"33cc6ae4-d96b-4585-a547-7780468d290c","component":"Text","data":{"content":"After fixing one particularly annoying path issue I hadn’t considered, the first version was published using GitHub Pages. CodeFolio is simple.  Developers deserve a better way to show how they think, not just what code they write.  Repositories are great for code.  CodeFolio exists to explain everything around the code.  And if it also happens to make that information easier for humans, AI systems, and future hiring processes to understand…  Even better.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]}]
